author: @Bart851
text:
I work at a supermarket and noticed something - any Disney merch sold is always of majorly the OG stuff. Classic 2D animations. It feels like Disney might actually be aware their remakes are trash since their major prority is the marketability, but since corporations have a major trouble with swallowing their pride and admitting their mistakes, they keep going. There is barely any (if any at all) merch related to the Disney remakes found commonly. 
Imagine this situation - present two backpacks to a 7-year old girl. One features the image of the classic Snow White and the other has Rachel Zegler's smug mug. The girl would most likely the former without hesitation and would've been bullied for wearing the latter at school.

replies.text:
The plot makes no sense within the world whatsoever. It has to be one of the worst-written scripts I have ever had the misfortune to see. Like, even in the first part, the evil Queen cuts Snow White’s hair to “make her ugly”, but right in front of a bunch of girls who have super long hair, and the Evil Queen does nothing to those girls. And it’s not like the other girls are ugly or anything. It makes no sense. 

I don’t even think the director cared about the script.

author: @robinwestlake4761
text: I know this is a long shot, but I made a movie in 2020 called VENOM (no not that one) which was interrupted by the plague and lockdown, costing me sets and cast. I decided to finish it in 2020 no matter what. The result was an abomination with poster backdrops, cast doubling up in wigs and prosthetics, a toy van with LCD monitor rear projection. It is on Amazon Prime, Xumo, Tubi and a few other channels. I would be delighted if you would cover it on your channel and see if it fares better than Snow White. It would be the first time the person responsible for the film invited the roast.

author: @Triviate
text: The bandits are very easy to explain. They WERE the "dwarves" role initially in the script. But backlash was so severe about them and they already had contracts for the movie, so they were reused and recast as this extra "bandit" role and the cgi dwarves were added to fill the gap. It's why the dwarves barely mattered to the conclusion of the plot compared to the bandits. That is my take anyway.

author: @TROOPERfarcry
text:
She claims that the prince basically stalks her.... and yet her character broke into a home of disabled people and immediately started bossing them around.

EDITED: a number of people are commenting that dwarfism isn't a disability -- which it technically is -- or that the dwarves in this story are a fictional race, and not the same as a human with dwarfism.  These people are correct.  _HOWEVER,_ they are missing the point of my original comment.  Rachel Ziegler analyzed the prince through a real-life and modern standards... so I analyzed her character through those same standards, and exposed how her character is no better, and arguably worse.  That's it.  Arguing one side while ignoring the other is the mistake that she made, and people arguing about dwarves vs dwarfism are making the *exact same mistake.*

author: @milotin
text:
In the 1937 movie, Snow White finds the dwarves cottage, thinks that children live there, but they must be orphans because they had no mother to clean up after them, so she decides to surprise them by cleaning their house and maybe they will let her stay.

So originally, she's kind, somewhat motherly, and knows she is imposing by being in their home. 

In 2025, Rachel orders the dwarves to clean up the house so she can live there comfortably. Way to go Disney.

replies.author: @Attmay
replies.text: @loutlander I don't feel good about Disney taking a dump on its animated legacy. Literally for those who suffered through this exact same BS with the dreadful *Pinocchio* remake. Tom Hanks is about as Italian as a peanut butter and jelly sandwich, and nobody called his miscasting as Geppetto "cultural appropriation." But he was almost as bad there as he was at playing Walt Disney. There's no cringe quite like Disney cringe. No one else has quite the right combination of stomach-churning eepy-creepy falsity and forehead-slapping stupidity.

replies.author: @schwaaard
replies.text: @Attmay I think many of us (or maybe it's just me) have had a rude awakening to what Hollywood has always been. We see it clearly now, but this  isn't new. Yes, we have gotten some truly great films and TV shows, albeit with some minor contaminants thrown in here and there. But the industry itself has been unhealthy for a long time - perhaps at its foundation. We've seen rather horrendous miscasting over the years (including race-swaps that have gone the 'other' way), often justified by the need for star power (as they define 'star'). All the seepage we recoil at now is just the process of everything that's been piped in for decades finally coming to the surface. I'm all about celebrating the worthwhile projects that somehow emerged from such a dysfunctional industry, but the veneer has been stripped away. Snow White and early Disney had true beauty in both the art and the stories, but I'd say as early as The Little Mermaid, we started to see that the core was beginning to rot. After Disney passed, the studio went into remission for over a decade, and TLM was considered part of a renaissance. Disney became a name again, but it didn't have the same values, tone, or wholesomeness. Few saw it at the time, but where we are now is just an extension of the 'new' Disney that began in '89.

replies.author: @Attmay
replies.text: @schwaaard boomers destroyed the Magic Kingdom in order to "save" it, just like Vietnam.

replies.author: @billjacobs521
replies.text: @schwaaard I strongly disagree. The renaissance films absolutely had the tone and wholesomeness, that's why they did so well for a period there. This slop is nothing like that.

replies.author: @schwaaard
replies.text: @ I agree that the production quality was amazing, and I enjoyed them as a kid. But as an adult I don't think a lot of the characters age well. Glad you like them though!

author: @carlrood4457
text:
I think the main problem with the whole "girl boss" movement is that it ALWAYS diminishes the story and characters because they refuse to show weakness.  

Did Luke Skywalker need help?   YES, a lot of it.  Obi Wan taught him the Force, Han and Chewie distracted Darth Vader, Han saved him on Hoth, Leia and Lando rescued him on Bespin.  Everyone had a role to play in rescuing Han from Jabba.  

Harry Potter needed a ton of help from friends and teachers.  He had a few things he was talented at, like riding a broom, and quite a few things he wasn't good at.  Ron, Hermione, Dumbledore, Snape, the Weasleys, Lupin, etc. ALL played a significant role in enabling Harry's victory.  Hell, even Lockhart helped by teaching him Expelliarmus.

The point is that male role models overcome their weaknesses and fears.  They need the help of others.  Girl Bosses are treated like they're not only perfect, but they're MORE PERFECT than they'd previously believed.  They're not just special.  They SUPER DUPER EXTRA SPECIAL.

replies.author: @Attmay
replies.text: @julia-nu4fj and what's all the more astonishing about it is that it came out during the depths of the great depression. That's why it was such a big financial gamble for Walt Disney to make that movie, but one that paid off more handsomely than Prince Charming himself. Many people thought that the idea of an entire feature film of nothing but animation would be too much for an audience to take. But they were wrong, and Walt knew it. Not only did he prove those naysayers wrong, he made them want more and more like it. And so the tradition of the Disney animated feature began. And within two years of it, the world would be at war, so much of the world wouldn't even get to see the next features from Walt until after the war. But his first five features set a standard that even this studio struggled to equal in subsequent years. Now, they just don't care. The naysayers are the ones running the studio now, and what they have created is everything wrong with all the other remakes, only worse because this is the most openly contemptuous of the source material whether it is the Grimm Brothers or the Nine Old Men. None of the changes are to make it more faithful to the original. They are just more gaslighting, gatekeeping, and girlbullying like all the others.

replies.author: @Attmay
replies.text: @TruthTellert63 the boomers who were basically Walt's surrogate grandchildren ignored every lesson of his and rebelled against them. They subverted Disney and turned it from a family-oriented animation studio and theme park company to the Monsanto of show business. I used to get excited for new Disney movies. I used to take my whole family to see them. Now, you couldn't pay me to see the slop they slap the Disney name onto today.
